Let me re-read the instructions: "For each feature in Wave 1 that does not need a worktree, spawn a parallel Agent call." So I should skip features that need a worktree.

But wait, the wave only has one feature and it needs a worktree. 

Since the only feature in Wave 1 requires a worktree, there are no features that meet the criteria for parallel execution, so there's nothing to spawn. I see the Agent tool supports an `isolation: "worktree"` parameter that creates a temporary isolated copy of the repo, which might be the right approach here. The user's instruction to skip features that don't need worktrees suggests I should handle worktree-requiring features differently—maybe by using that parameter instead of skipping them entirely.

getLatestMilestoneUatRun: (slug: string) =>
    request<import('@/lib/types').UatRun | null>(`/api/milestones/${encodeURIComponent(slug)}/uat-runs/latest`),
  uatArtifactUrl: (milestoneSlug: string, runId: string, filename: string): string =>
    `/api/milestones/${encodeURIComponent(milestoneSlug)}/uat-runs/${encodeURIComponent(runId)}/artifacts/${encodeURIComponent(filename)}`,

  getConfig: () => request<import('@/lib/types').ProjectConfig>('/api/config'),
